runs.text: The true
U-value
of walls that have already been insulated. The standard figure for the building's age can be wrong here.
Whether an upgrade suits your building type. Cavity wall insulation, for example, does not work for every cavity wall.
The assessor may have logged the wall type wrong. To challenge it, contact the Energy Performance of Buildings Register and ask for a re-assessment.
The home's design heat loss. The emitter output at the planned flow temperature. Radiator makers list output data for different mean water temperatures.

A house with original-size radiators that has not been upgraded may not stay warm below 50 degrees Celsius. In that case, check the radiator output at the design outdoor temperature against the room heat loss. If the gap is large, the radiators need upgrading before the flow temperature can drop. Some listed buildings limit how radiators can look. There, use electric underfloor heating mats in single rooms instead of cutting the flow temperature.
Shading assessment: a formal shading study using Solmetric, PV Sol, or similar. Not just a look by eye.
Full payment is required before the install starts. The industry norm is a deposit when you sign, staged payments during the work, and the final payment once the system is switched on and signed off.

Run an equalisation charge every 6 months, or right after a long spell of partial discharge. Set the charge controller to the equalisation voltage. This is typically 15.5 to 16.2V for a 12V flooded bank; check the maker's spec. Leave the battery tops open and make sure there is ventilation, as equalisation gives off hydrogen gas. Run it for 2 to 8 hours, until the electrolyte gasses evenly across all cells and the specific gravity readings settle. Do not equalise sealed AGM or gel batteries; it will damage them. Never equalise lithium batteries.
Moderate mobile signal: an external aimed antenna on a 4G router usually gives a steady link. Test with a hotspot first, before you buy a router and SIM deal.
Heating: any solid fuel stove, biomass boiler, wood gasifier, or heating system without a certificate. For solid fuel, a HETAS certificate is useful proof.
Escape of water from private supply systems: many policies only cover water escape from a mains-fed system. Check if yours covers burst pipes or tank overflow on a private supply.
Unoccupied property clauses: many policies cut cover after 30 or 60 days empty in a row. Second homes and holiday lets need their own empty-property wording.

Over-rafter work needs scaffolding and a roofer to lift and relay the tiles. Tell Building Control before any major energy upgrade under Part L1B. For spans over 4 m, get the screw spec from the insulation maker or a structural engineer.
Make a set of three frames in graded sizes. Use the same flower layout in each one for a wall group.
For a change from cacti, make succulent-style pieces. Use flat, layered petals that spread out from a central point.
Larger scale: size the wall former up to 80 mm per side. This gives a bigger house that works as a centrepiece in a fairy garden planter.

Modern string inverters have shade-tolerant algorithms, and some have more than one MPPT input. A dual-MPPT inverter can split the array into two separate strings. It copes with mismatched orientation, but not with local shading inside one string. CREST modelling shows that one panel shaded by 20 percent, in a 10-panel string, can cut total output by 8 to 35 percent. The figure depends on the inverter's shade algorithm. The wide range comes from differences in bypass diode setup and shade handling.

A vet can prescribe antibiotics for acute Pasteurella. Common choices are enrofloxacin or trimethoprim-sulphonamide. These control the infection but do not clear it, so the rabbit stays a carrier. In a meat system, repeated treatment is rarely worth it. Talk the decision over with your vet.

Use the retailer's packaging settings where they exist. On eligible items, Amazon offers 'Frustration-Free Packaging' and 'No Rush Shipping with sustainability credit'. John Lewis, Marks and Spencer, and Patagonia all offer reduced packaging too. Check the choices at the checkout.

Skip express or next-day delivery when you do not need it. Express dispatch puts speed first, so items from the same depot get sent on their own. Standard delivery lets the warehouse batch them together.
The loft is very dry and has good cross-ventilation. Here, moisture buffering gives no real benefit.

Before bringing in replacement animals, clean and disinfect all housing, feeders, drinkers, and handling gear. The order matters: clear out all muck before you apply disinfectant. Disinfectant cannot get through dried manure; it only treats the surface. Pressure-wash first, let it dry, then apply an approved disinfectant at the stated rate.

Many lofts were foam-insulated under ECO1 or Green Deal schemes that came with guarantees. Contact the original installer or scheme operator first. The National Insulation Association runs a consumer protection scheme. Some cases have been settled under those warranties at no cost to the owner.
Large north-facing windows in a well-insulated new build. Here the glazing is the main surface losing heat.

Listed buildings and conservation areas: you usually need planning permission to replace original windows. Secondary glazing is normally allowed without consent. It can be removed and sits on the inside.

The frames are in good shape but the glass is cold: the frame is sound, yet single-glazed or old double-glazed. Adding secondary glazing doubles the glazing's thermal resistance, and leaves the frame untouched.

Hinged aluminium frame with safety glass: a full secondary unit on inward hinges, with a slim aluminium frame. It lets you open the main window fully for air. Cost per window: 250 to 600 pounds, fitted by a pro.

Warm roof work needs a Building Regulations application when you create a habitable room. Both the structural sums and the fire-resistance specs need sign-off. Cold loft insulation needs no consent, unless it disturbs the existing structure.

Above 300 mg/l hardness: an ion-exchange softener is likely worth it if you protect a combi boiler, radiators, and appliances. Payback is 3 to 5 years at today's salt prices, set against descaling and replacement costs.
Low-modulus silicone or polyurethane sealant. Pick a colour to match the frame or wall.
High-modulus sealant was used instead of low-modulus. Replace it with a flexible low-modulus silicone or polyurethane. The joint needs to move; a rigid filler is the problem.
Flats with shared walls: the EWI contractor needs a party wall agreement. This applies if the work reaches the outer face of the party wall.

Solid brick versus stone: flint, rubble limestone, and Cotswold stone each have a different surface. Adhesive amounts differ. Do a dry-brush test on a 1 m² patch before the full job to check the substrate suits. See
EWI installation
for the substrate assessment steps.
Black walnut: it contains juglone, a natural compound that holds back growth in many vegetables and ornamentals. The effect lasts even after partial composting.
